title: NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 Power Consumption: High-Density Colocation in 2026 | GoDataCenters posted on the topic | LinkedIn
text:
The latest NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 racks draw 120-130 kW. Traditional air cooling was designed for 8-12 kW per rack. Our latest analysis breaks down what enterprise buyers need to know about high-density colocation in 2026: → Direct liquid cooling vs. immersion comparison → 5 dimensions for evaluating a provider's cooling roadmap → 20-point AI readiness checklist → Why 90% of existing facilities cannot support AI workloads Use GoDataCenters' AI-Ready filter to find facilities built for high-density workloads. https://lnkd.in/gEbBDGRX

      A simple shift in thinking 👇 AI success is not about adding more compute. It’s about optimizing the entire environment. From data to storage to orchestration, every layer matters. Great insight from Philippa Carroll at #NVIDIAGTC. #AIInfrastructure #DDN #NVIDIA #AI More GPUs are not the whole story. 📖 At the DDN booth during NVIDIA GTC 2026, RAVEL CPO Philippa Carroll shared a message the industry needs to hear: AI success comes from how well every part of the stack works together. From compute to data systems, storage, and orchestration, real AI infrastructure performance depends on optimization across the entire environment. Watch the clip ⬇️ To view or add a comment, sign in

      GOING LIVE TOMORROW 🔥 Why does GPU utilization remain low even as infrastructure investment keeps growing? In multi-tenant AI platforms, visibility is limited. Cluster management is fragmented. GPUs sit underutilized while costs continue to rise. Without platform-level coordination, efficiency is inconsistent and difficult to measure. In this webinar, Maximizing GPU Utilization: Turning Multi-Tenant AI Infrastructure into a Margin Engine, we show how leading teams identify utilization gaps and move beyond basic GPU-as-a-Service models. We also cover how GPU-aware orchestration and centralized control improve efficiency at scale.
